author: jñānadāsa
verses:
pūrave govardhana, dharala anuja jā̐’ra,
jagajane kahe balarāma
ebe se caitanya⋅saṅge, āilā kīrtana⋅raṅge,
dhari’ pa̐hu nityānanda nāma

parama udāra, karuṇāmaya vigraha,
bhuvanamaṅgala guṇa⋅dhāma
gaura⋅pīriti⋅rase, kaṭira vasana khase,
avatāra ati anupama

nācata gāota, hari hari bolata,
avirata gaura⋅gopāla
hāsa⋅prakāśa, milita madhurādhare,
bolata parama rasāla

rāmadāsera pa̐hu, sundara vigraha,
gaurīdāsera dhana⋅prāṇe
akhila jīva jata, iha rase unamata,
jñānadāsa nitāi guṇagāne
en_translation:
In the past, His younger brother held Govardhana Hill. The people of the world call Him Balarāma. Now, He has come with Lord Caitanya in the joy of congregational chanting, assuming the name of Lord Nityānanda.

He is supremely magnanimous, the embodiment of mercy, and the abode of auspicious qualities for the whole universe. In the nectar of love for Lord Gaura, His waist-cloth loosens. He is a most incomparable incarnation.

He dances and sings, chanting "Hari! Hari!" incessantly for Lord Gaura-Gopāla. A radiant smile adorns His sweet lips, and He speaks words full of supreme nectar.

He is the Master of Rāmadāsa, a beautiful form, and the wealth and life of Gaurīdāsa Paṇḍita. All the living entities in the universe become intoxicated in this mellow. Jñānadāsa sings the glories of Lord Nitāi.
